pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies query name as 'EmployeeHierarchy'

The specified path does not exist on the system, but the Chinook database is a publicly available sample database from https://github.com/lerocha/chinook-database. File verified to be 1.1 MB SQLite database.

Downloaded Chinook_Sqlite.sqlite v1.4.5 from GitHub and executed self-join query: 'SELECT e.FirstName || ' ' || e.LastName AS EmployeeName, e.Title AS EmployeeTitle, m.FirstName || ' ' || m.LastName AS ManagerName, m.Title AS ManagerTitle FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Employee m ON e.ReportsTo = m.EmployeeId ORDER BY e.EmployeeId'. Result: 8 rows (one for each of 8 employees in the Employee table).

Executed self-join query on Chinook database and verified column names returned match exactly: EmployeeName (employee FirstName || LastName), EmployeeTitle (e.Title), ManagerName (manager FirstName || LastName), ManagerTitle (m.Title).

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a well-known sample database containing 8 employees. A self-join query with LEFT JOIN on the Employee table returns exactly 8 rows with columns: EmployeeName, EmployeeTitle, ManagerName, and ManagerTitle. The top-level employee (Andrew Adams, General Manager) has no manager (NULL), while all other 7 employees report to various managers in the hierarchy. The expected query name is 'EmployeeHierarchy'. All expected column names and row count have been verified against the actual Chinook database (v1.4.5).
